input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Tube/Tubing/Tubes`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Tube/Tubing/Tubes` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: f.3.4.1
            TITLE: minimum requirements for steel tubing

            CONTENT:
            rule F.3.4.1: Minimum Requirements for Steel Tubing
F.3.4.1 Minimum Requirements for Steel Tubing 
A tube must meet all four minimum requirements for each Size specified: 
 Tube 
Minimum 
Area 
Moment of 
Inertia 
Minimum 
Cross 
Sectional 
Area 
Minimum 
Outside 
Diameter or 
Square Width 
Minimum 
Wall  
Thickness 
Example Sizes of 
Round Tube 
 
a. Size A 11320 mm4 173 mm2 25.0 mm 2.0 mm 1.0” x 0.095” 
25 x 2.5 mm 
 
b. Size B 8509 mm4 114 mm2 25.0 mm 1.2 mm 1.0” x 0.065” 
25.4 x 1.6 mm 
 
c. Size C 6695 mm4 91 mm2 25.0 mm 1.2 mm 1.0” x 0.049” 
25.4 x 1.2 mm 
 
d. Size D 18015 mm4 126 mm2 35.0 mm 1.2 mm 1.375” x 0.049” 
35 x 1.2 mm
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 27 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: f.5.1.2
            TITLE: any chassis design that combines the tube frame, monocoque, tubing and/or composite

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.1.2: Any chassis design that combines the Tube Frame, Monocoque, tubing and/or composite
F.5.1.2 Any chassis design that combines the Tube Frame, Monocoque, tubing and/or composite 
types must: 
a. Meet all relevant requirements F.5.1.1 
b. Show Equivalence F.2.3, as applicable  
c. Any connections must meet F.5.4, F.5.5, F.7.8 as applicable, or Equivalent.

RULE: f.5.1.1
            TITLE: the primary structure must be constructed from one or a combination of the following:

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.1.1: The Primary Structure must be constructed from one or a combination of the following:
F.5.1.1 The Primary Structure must be constructed from one or a combination of the following: 
• Steel Tubing and Material  F.3.2   F.3.4 
• Alternative Tubing Materials F.3.2 F.3.5 
• Composite Material  F.4

RULE: f.3.2.1
            TITLE: requirements by application

            CONTENT:
            rule F.3.2.1: Requirements by Application
F.3.2.1 Requirements by Application 
 Application 
Steel Tube Must 
Meet Size per 
F.3.4: 
Alternative Tubing 
Material Permitted 
per F.3.5 ? 
 
a. Front Bulkhead Size B Yes  
b. Front Bulkhead Support Size C Yes  
c. Front Hoop Size A Yes  
d. Front Hoop Bracing Size B Yes  
e. Side Impact Structure Size B Yes  
f. Bent / Multi Upper Side Impact Member Size D Yes  
g. Main Hoop Size A NO  
h. Main Hoop Bracing Size B NO  
i. Main Hoop Bracing Supports Size C Yes  
j. Driver Restraint Harness Attachment Size B Yes  
k. Shoulder Harness Mounting Bar Size A NO  
l. Shoulder Harness Mounting Bar Bracing Size C Yes  
m. Accumulator Protection Structure Size B Yes  
n. Component Protection Size C Yes  
o. Structural Tubing Size C Yes

RULE: f.2.3.2
            TITLE: any equivalency calculations must prove equivalency relative to steel tubing in the same

            CONTENT:
            rule F.2.3.2: Any Equivalency calculations must prove Equivalency relative to Steel Tubing in the same
F.2.3.2 Any Equivalency calculations must prove Equivalency relative to Steel Tubing in the same 
application

RULE: f.5.15
            TITLE: other side tube requirements

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.15: Other Side Tube Requirements
F.5.15 Other Side Tube Requirements  
If there is a Roll Hoop Brace or other frame tube alongside the driver, at the height of the neck 
of any of the team’s drivers, a metal tube or piece of sheet metal must be attached to the 
Frame 
This is intended to prevent the drivers’ shoulders from passing under the Roll Hoop Brace or 
frame tube, and the driver’s neck contacting this brace or tube.

RULE: f.3.3.1
            TITLE: definition

            CONTENT:
            rule F.3.3.1: Definition
F.3.3.1 Definition 
Any tubing which does NOT meet F.3.2.1.o Structural Tubing

RULE: f.3.4.3
            TITLE: where welded tubing reinforcements are required (such as inserts for bolt holes or material

            CONTENT:
            rule F.3.4.3: Where Welded tubing reinforcements are required (such as inserts for bolt holes or material
F.3.4.3 Where Welded tubing reinforcements are required (such as inserts for bolt holes or material 
to support suspension cutouts), Equivalence of the Welded tube and reinforcement must be 
shown to the original Non Welded tube in the SES

RULE: f.5.3.3
            TITLE: regulated tubing other than the open lower ends of roll hoops must have any open ends

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.3.3: Regulated tubing other than the open lower ends of Roll Hoops must have any open ends
F.5.3.3 Regulated tubing other than the open lower ends of Roll Hoops must have any open ends 
closed by a welded cap or inserted metal plug.

RULE: f.3.3.2
            TITLE: applicability

            CONTENT:
            rule F.3.3.2: Applicability
F.3.3.2 Applicability 
Non Structural Tubing is ignored when assessing compliance to any rule

RULE: f.5.6.2
            TITLE: the main hoop and front hoop must be triangulated into the primary structure with

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.6.2: The Main Hoop and Front Hoop must be Triangulated into the Primary Structure with
F.5.6.2 The Main Hoop and Front Hoop must be Triangulated into the Primary Structure with 
Structural Tubing  
The Triangulation must be at a node in side view for: 
a. Bends in side view 
b. Bends in front view below the Upper Side Impact Structure  F.6.4, F.7.5

RULE: f.5.2.3
            TITLE: if a bent tube (or member consisting of multiple tubes that are not in a line) is used anywhere

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.2.3: If a bent tube (or member consisting of multiple tubes that are not in a line) is used anywhere
F.5.2.3 If a bent tube (or member consisting of multiple tubes that are not in a line) is used anywhere 
in the Primary Structure other than the Roll Hoops (see F.5.6.2), an additional tube must be 
attached to support it.   
a. The support tube attachment point must be at the position along the bent tube where it 
deviates farthest from a straight line connecting both ends  
b. The support tube must terminate at a node of the chassis 
c. The support tube for any bent tube (other than the Upper Side Impact Member or 
Shoulder Harness Mounting Bar) must be: 
• The same diameter and thickness as the bent tube 
• Angled no more than 30° from the plane of the bent tube

RULE: f.5.8.1
            TITLE: the main hoop must be a single piece of uncut, continuous, closed section steel tubing

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.8.1: The Main Hoop must be a single piece of uncut, continuous, closed section steel tubing
F.5.8.1 The Main Hoop must be a single piece of uncut, continuous, closed section steel tubing 
meeting F.3.2.1.g

RULE: f.6.2.4
            TITLE: each of the above members may be multiple or bent tubes provided the requirements of f.5.2

            CONTENT:
            rule F.6.2.4: Each of the above members may be multiple or bent tubes provided the requirements of F.5.2
F.6.2.4 Each of the above members may be multiple or bent tubes provided the requirements of F.5.2 
are met.

RULE: f.5.12.4
            TITLE: all double lug joints, whether fitted parallel or perpendicular to the axis of the tube, must

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.12.4: All Double Lug Joints, whether fitted parallel or perpendicular to the axis of the tube, must
F.5.12.4 All Double Lug Joints, whether fitted parallel or perpendicular to the axis of the tube, must 
include a capping arrangement

RULE: f.3.5.3
            TITLE: aluminum tubing

            CONTENT:
            rule F.3.5.3: Aluminum Tubing
F.3.5.3 Aluminum Tubing  
a. Minimum Wall Thickness for Aluminum Tubing:  Non Welded 2.0 mm 
Welded  3.0 mm 
b. Non Welded properties for aluminum alloy 6061-T6 for calculations in an SES must be: 
Young’s Modulus (E)  69 GPa (10,000 ksi) 
Yield Strength (Sy)  240 MPa (34.8 ksi) 
Ultimate Strength (Su)  290 MPa (42.1 ksi) 
c. Welded properties for aluminum alloy 6061-T6 for calculations in an SES must be: 
Yield Strength (Sy) 115 MPa (16.7 ksi) 
Ultimate Strength (Su) 175 MPa (25.4 ksi) 
d. If welding is used on a regulated aluminum structure, the equivalent yield strength must 
be considered in the “as welded” condition for the alloy used unless the team provides 
detailed proof that the frame or component has been properly solution heat treated, 
artificially aged, and not subject to heating during team manufacturing.   
e. If aluminum was solution heat treated and age hardened to increase its strength after 
welding, the team must supply evidence as to how the process was performed.  
This includes, but is not limited to, the heat treating facility used, the process applied, 
and the fixturing used.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 28 of 140

RULE: f.3.5.3
            TITLE: aluminum tubing

            CONTENT:
            rule F.3.5.3: Aluminum Tubing
This includes, but is not limited to, the heat treating facility used, the process applied, 
and the fixturing used.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 28 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: f.5.3.2
            TITLE: technical inspectors may check the compliance of all tubes.  this may be done by ultrasonic

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.3.2: Technical Inspectors may check the compliance of all tubes.  This may be done by ultrasonic
F.5.3.2 Technical Inspectors may check the compliance of all tubes.  This may be done by ultrasonic 
testing or by the drilling of inspection holes on request.

RULE: t.1.5.1
            TITLE: the driver’s seat must be protected by one of the following:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.5.1: The Driver’s Seat must be protected by one of the following:
T.1.5.1 The Driver’s Seat must be protected by one of the following: 
a. In side view, the lowest point of any Driver’s Seat must be no lower than the upper 
surface of the lowest structural tube or equivalent 
b. A longitudinal tube (or tubes) that meets the requirements for Side Impact tubing 
(F.3.2.1.e), passing underneath the lowest point of the Driver Seat.

RULE: f.5.8.3
            TITLE: in the side view of the vehicle,

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.8.3: In the side view of the vehicle,
F.5.8.3 In the side view of the vehicle,  
a. The part of the Main Hoop that lies above its attachment point to the upper Side Impact 
Tube must be less than 10° from vertical.  
b. Any bends in the Main Hoop above its attachment point to the Major Structure of the 
Chassis must be braced to a node or Attachment point F.7.8 with tubing meeting 
F.3.2.1.h and F.5.9.5  
c. The part of the Main Hoop below the Upper Side Impact Member attachment: 
• May be forward at any angle 
• Must not be rearward more than 10° from vertical

RULE: f.5.9.1
            TITLE: main hoop braces must be constructed of closed section steel tubing meeting f.3.2.1.h

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.9.1: Main Hoop Braces must be constructed of closed section steel tubing meeting F.3.2.1.h
F.5.9.1 Main Hoop Braces must be constructed of closed section steel tubing meeting F.3.2.1.h

RULE: f.1.2
            TITLE: frame member

            CONTENT:
            rule F.1.2: Frame Member
F.1.2 Frame Member 
A minimum representative single piece of uncut, continuous tubing.

RULE: f.2.3.3
            TITLE: the properties of tubes and laminates may be combined to prove equivalence.

            CONTENT:
            rule F.2.3.3: The properties of tubes and laminates may be combined to prove Equivalence.
F.2.3.3 The properties of tubes and laminates may be combined to prove Equivalence.  
For example, in a Side Impact Structure consisting of one tube per F.3.2.1.e and a laminate 
panel, the panel only needs to be Equivalent to two Side Impact Tubes.

RULE: ic.5.4.4
            TITLE: a clear fuel filler neck tube may be used as a sight tube, subject to approval by a rules

            CONTENT:
            rule IC.5.4.4: A clear Fuel Filler Neck tube may be used as a sight tube, subject to approval by a Rules
IC.5.4.4 A clear Fuel Filler Neck tube may be used as a sight tube, subject to approval by a Rules 
Question or technical inspectors at the event.

RULE: f.6.4.2
            TITLE: with proper triangulation, side impact structure members may be fabricated from more than

            CONTENT:
            rule F.6.4.2: With proper Triangulation, Side Impact Structure members may be fabricated from more than
F.6.4.2 With proper Triangulation, Side Impact Structure members may be fabricated from more than 
one piece of tubing.

RULE: f.5.16
            TITLE: component protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.5.16: Component Protection
F.5.16 Component Protection  
When specified in the rules, components must be protected by one or both of:  
a. Fully Triangulated structure with tubes meeting F.3.2.1.n  
b. Structure Equivalent to the above, as determined per F.4.1.3

RULE: d.2.3
            TITLE: powertrain operation

            CONTENT:
            rule D.2.3: Powertrain Operation
D.2.3 Powertrain Operation 
In the paddock, (IC) Engines may be run or (EV) Tractive System may be Active if all three:   
a. The vehicle has passed Technical Inspection up to and including the Tilt Test OR a 
Technical Inspector gives permission  
b. The vehicle is supported on a stand  
c. The drive wheels are minimum 10 cm off the ground, OR the drive wheels are removed
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 126 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: t.2.3.1
            TITLE: vehicles with a reclined driving position must have:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.3.1: Vehicles with a Reclined Driving Position must have:
T.2.3.1 Vehicles with a Reclined Driving Position must have: 
a. A 6 Point Harness or a 7 Point Harness 
b. Anti-Submarine Belts with tilt lock adjusters (“quick adjusters”) OR two sets of Anti-
Submarine Belts installed.

RULE: in.8.3.2
            TITLE: when the front hoop is integrally bonded or laminated to the monocoque f.7.4.3, provide:

            CONTENT:
            rule IN.8.3.2: When the Front Hoop is integrally bonded or laminated to the monocoque F.7.4.3, provide:
IN.8.3.2 When the Front Hoop is integrally bonded or laminated to the monocoque F.7.4.3, provide:  
a. Documentation that shows dimensions on the tubes 
b. Pictures of the dimensioned tube being included in the layup

RULE: t.1.6.3
            TITLE: the design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.6.3: The design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples
T.1.6.3 The design must address all three types of heat transfer between the heat source (examples 
include but are not limited to:  exhaust pipe, coolant hose/tube, Accumulator Container) and 
the panel that the driver could contact (seat or floor): 
a. Conduction Isolation by one of the following: 
• No direct contact between the heat source and the panel 
• A heat resistant, conduction isolation material with a minimum thickness of 8 mm 
between the heat source and the panel  
b. Convection Isolation by a minimum air gap of 25 mm between the heat source and the 
panel 
c. Radiation Isolation by one of the following: 
• A solid metal heat shield with a minimum thickness of 0.4 mm 
• Reflective foil or tape when combined with conduction insulation
